artifacts.content:
From: maria.thompson89.ak@gmail.com
To: jess.miller.ak@gmail.com
Subject: Completely wiped after this week

Jess,

I’m finally sitting down on the couch with some tea and my legs feel like jelly. This week with Helen plus kid drama just steamrolled me.

Friday (1/9) at her place on Alder View was scary. Her blood sugar crashed to 46 while I was helping her to the bathroom. I ended up staying about 25 minutes past when I was supposed to leave because I was on the phone with her endocrinologist, Dr. Patel at Alaska Regional Hospital, for close to half an hour while we adjusted her insulin and pushed juice. I didn’t even think about the time until after she was stable and back in her recliner.

Then Sunday 1/11 at around 8 a.m. at Helen’s unit, Daniel kept blowing up my phone about the 1/22 custody hearing at the Anchorage Courthouse. He said his lawyer wants to push for reduced visitation with Olivia and Ethan. I was so upset I tapped the wrong thing in my work app and had to basically start over. It’s like he waits until I’m at work to pick a fight.

On top of that, Ethan had another asthma flare on 1/10 and Dad took him to Providence Alaska Medical Center while I was still with Helen. Olivia texted me in a panic and I had to step into the hallway for about 10 minutes to call and make sure they were okay before I could focus again.

I keep thinking I need to figure out some better boundaries, but it all piles on at once: clients, kids, courts, bills. Anyway, thanks for letting me dump this out. How’s your weekend looking? Want to come over for soup Sunday night?

Love,
Maria

From: maria.thompson89.ak@gmail.com
To: caroline.miller.ak@example.com
Subject: That UAA bill + rough day at Helen’s

Mom,

I needed to write this out before I try to sleep. Today (1/13) at Helen’s building on Alder View Drive just broke me a little.

Around 3:40 p.m., while Helen was resting in her chair, I checked my phone and saw an email from the University of Alaska Anchorage billing office. They’re still chasing that $1,842 I owe from the 2013 Allied Health program. Seeing the exact number again – $1,842 – made my stomach drop. I ended up sitting in the stairwell doing those breathing exercises you taught me, because I could feel a full-on panic attack coming. I think I was out there about 15 minutes before I could pull myself together enough to finish documenting and head home.

All of this is hitting while I’m trying to think about maybe starting LPN prerequisites in fall 2026. It’s hard to picture paying for new classes when I haven’t even cleared the old balance.

On top of that, Helen had a hard moment today when we drove past Providence Alaska Medical Center on the way back from groceries. It reminded her of that COPD hospitalization last October. She grabbed my arm and kept repeating what the ER doctor, Dr. Larkin, told her about maybe not making it through another pneumonia. She calmed down after about 20 minutes back in her apartment, especially after talking to Sarah, but she’s more scared about going out now.

Anyway, I’ll call you tomorrow, but I wanted you to know why I was so quiet tonight.

Love you,
Maria

metadata.subject: That UAA bill + rough day at Helen’s

artifacts.content:
Thread with: Daniel R. Thompson
Date: 2026-01-12

Maria: Hey, quick update about yesterday and this morning.

Maria: First, Dad and Olivia handled Ethan’s asthma thing on 1/10 – they took him to the ER at Providence Alaska Medical Center. I was still at Helen’s, so I stepped into the hallway for about 10 minutes to talk to them, but he’s okay now. They adjusted his inhaler schedule a bit.

Maria: Second, I really need you to stop calling nonstop when I’m with clients. On Sunday 1/11 at like 8:05 a.m. at Helen Morrison’s unit in the Alder View complex, you rang over and over about the 1/22/2026 custody hearing at the Anchorage Courthouse and your lawyer “pushing for reduced visitation” with Olivia and Ethan. I got so upset I hit the wrong thing in my work app and had to redo it.

Maria: This morning (1/12) I was 7 minutes late checking in at Helen’s because my GCI data ran out right as I pulled up. I had to throw $30 on my prepaid plan with my debit card in the parking lot before the app would even open. I’m already behind on utilities since you stopped sending the full child support back in November 2025. I can’t keep plugging holes like this.

Maria: I’m asking you, please, keep calls about court and money to evenings when I’m home and the kids are settled. I need to stay focused when I’m helping Helen.

Daniel: Fine. We’ll talk after the kids are in bed tonight.

metadata.title: Text thread with Daniel about calls & money

artifacts.content:
Title: Work/mental load check-in – mid-January
Date: 2026-01-18

This past week with Helen (1/11–1/17) was “officially” stable, but my brain and body feel anything but.

Helen herself: mobility about the same. We did the usual positioning, transfers, PROM, walking laps with the four-wheel walker. Her osteoarthritis pain started around 4/10 in the mornings and usually eased to 2–3/10 after stretching. I kept checking her lower legs and heels – still just mild ankle swelling, no redness or open areas. Oxygen use stayed at 2 L/min via nasal cannula as needed, mostly mornings. No wheezing or chest pain, just that “little winded” feeling after walking from bedroom to kitchen Tuesday that went away with rest.

Emotionally, more going on. On 1/13 she panicked when we passed Providence Alaska Medical Center, remembering her COPD hospitalization last October and what Dr. Larkin told her about maybe not surviving another pneumonia. Took ~20 minutes of breathing exercises and a call to Sarah to calm her. On 1/16, while I was helping her in the bathroom, she admitted she’s been skipping her afternoon Lasix for three days because she’s terrified of “peeing myself in front of Maria again like I did in November when the toilet clogged.” I noticed her ankles looked a bit more swollen. I tried to gently explain why the med matters and encouraged her to talk to her nurse at Alaska Native Primary Care Center and her daughter, even though she asked me not to.

Meanwhile I’m juggling my own stuff: UAA’s email about the $1,842 balance from 2013, the 1/22 custody hearing hanging over me, Ethan’s asthma, and money stress. Need to remember to breathe, drink water, and maybe schedule one quiet evening just for myself this week.

From: maria.thompson89.ak@gmail.com
To: supervisor.carecoord@cdcn-ak.com
Subject: Update on Helen M. – mood, meds, safety

Hi [Name],

I wanted to share a few things I’ve noticed with Helen Morrison over the past week or so, in case it’s helpful for care coordination.

1) Mood/kidneys: After her primary care visit at Alaska Regional Hospital on 1/14/26 with NP Kelly Andrews, Helen has been noticeably quieter during our later shifts. She told me Kelly said her kidney function has “dropped a little more” and that they might need to talk about dialysis if it keeps worsening. She’s mentioned twice that she’s scared of “ending up like my sister on dialysis at the Fresenius clinic.” I’ve been trying to keep her engaged with small tasks and conversation.

2) Medication adherence: On 1/16, while I was assisting her in the bathroom, she confided that she has been skipping her afternoon Lasix for the last three days because she’s afraid of another incontinence episode like the one back in November when the toilet clogged and she had an accident in front of me. I did notice her ankle swelling looked a bit worse that evening. I encouraged her to be honest with her daughter and the nurse at Alaska Native Primary Care Center, and reminded her why the medication is important.

3) Safety/anxiety: She’s also been more jumpy at night since New Year’s Eve, when her neighbor Mark Jensen in Apt 2A was arrested in the parking lot for DUI and a probation violation. She told me on 1/15 she’s had more trouble sleeping and now double-checks her deadbolt and asks me to look out the hallway window before I leave.

Let me know if you’d like me to document anything specific during upcoming visits.

Best,
Maria L. Thompson
PCS / Home Care Aide
Employee ID: AK-473829

metadata.subject: Update on Helen M. – mood, meds, safety

artifacts.content:
Title: Week of 1/11–1/17 – Helen visits + kids + court stuff

Dates: 2026-01-11 to 2026-01-17
Location: Mostly Alder View / home / parents’ house
Attendees: Maria, Helen, Olivia, Ethan, Mom & Dad, etc.

Notes:

Sun 1/11
- 6:15 a.m. – up, pack lunches, check ASD weather.
- 9:00 a.m.–12:00 p.m. – PCS shift w/ Helen J. Morrison (CL-209517) at Alder View. Focus: positioning, PROM, transfers, hygiene, light meal, meds, oxygen 2 L/min as needed. Remember she was a little more anxious about going out after October COPD stay at Providence.
- Evening – kids’ homework, simple soup for dinner.

Mon 1/12
- 8:45 a.m. – drive to Helen’s; GCI data top-up ($30) in parking lot when app wouldn’t load, 7-min late check-in.
- After work – call GCI about cheaper plan; pay minimum on credit card.

Tue 1/13
- 2:00–6:00 p.m. – PCS shift w/ Helen (shopping + main meal). Grocery + pharmacy run; pass Providence Alaska Medical Center on the way back (watch for anxiety). She got tearful last time remembering Dr. Larkin saying she “might not make it through another pneumonia.”
- Around 3:40 p.m. – don’t open stressful emails at work (UAA billing email about $1,842 balance triggered panic attack in stairwell, 15-min delay).

Thu 1/15
- 9:30 a.m.–1:00 p.m. – PCS shift. Check mood, skin, ankles, oxygen. Ask gently about sleep since Mark Jensen’s New Year’s Eve DUI/probation arrest in parking lot.

Sat 1/17
- 10:00 a.m.–2:00 p.m. – PCS shift. Laundry, main meal, housework, oxygen safety, smoke detector check.
- Eve – kids to Mom & Dad’s; prep docs/notes for 1/22 custody hearing at Anchorage Courthouse.

metadata.title: Week of 1/11–1/17 – Helen visits + kids + court stuff

artifacts.content:
From: maria.thompson89.ak@gmail.com
To: jess.miller.ak@gmail.com
Subject: Little bright spots w/ Helen + Sunday plan

Hey Jess,

Wanted to tell you about a couple of small good things with Helen before I forget, and also see if you’re still up for bringing dessert to Mom and Dad’s.

On 1/12, her grandkids Emma and Lucas finally came by her apartment for about two hours. You should’ve seen her – totally lit up. Before that she kept saying since Christmas that she felt “forgotten” because Sarah’s been picking up so many shifts at the Costco on Dimond Boulevard and hadn’t brought the kids over. While they were here we helped her set up a weekly Sunday Zoom call using Sarah’s account, so I added it to my calendar to remind her.

She’s still rattled from New Year’s Eve when Mark Jensen from Apt 2A got arrested in the parking lot for a DUI and probation violation. She told me on 1/15 she hasn’t been sleeping well and now double-checks her deadbolt and asks me to peek out the hallway window before I leave. I can’t blame her, but I hate that she feels unsafe in her own building.

I’m also keeping an eye on her mood since that 1/14 visit with NP Kelly Andrews at Alaska Regional Hospital. Helen said her kidney numbers “dropped a little more” and they might talk dialysis if things keep sliding. She keeps saying she doesn’t want to “end up like my sister on dialysis at the Fresenius clinic.”

Anyway, those Sunday Zooms with Emma and Lucas might help. It reminded me how much Liv perks up after choir when you’re in the audience.

So for this Sunday: I’ll bring salmon and rice, can you handle dessert? Maybe that apple crisp the kids love?

Love,
Maria

metadata.subject: Little bright spots w/ Helen + Sunday plan
